subject: Fwd: Batch pre-processing in Warp and white lines across thon rings
body:
Dear Alister,

Although newstack and converting mrc to tif did not work, the IMOD command
'clip brightness -n 1 in.mrc out.mrc' (previously listed by Pranav Shah in
the thread you forwarded me) fixed the problem!

Thank you for your help!

Wish you a great day!

Best,

Cyan

PhD student at the Physical Chemistry Curie Lab

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com>
Date: Mon, 6 Dec 2021 at 19:40
Subject: Re: Batch pre-processing in Warp and white lines across thon rings
To: Cyan Ching <xingyichengcyanching@gmail.com>


Hi Cyan,

You still have a cut if you run the image through newstack? What I was
suggesting was simply to run it through newstack in.mrc out.mrc... I
haven’t had this problem myself but the problem is definitely something to
do with the image header, see here for people who’ve dealt with this before
https://groups.google.com/g/warp-em/c/YDTyETcqUF0/m/VVscoUa9AwAJ

Good luck, you’ll have this solved before you know it!

Alister

On 6 Dec 2021, at 16:25, Cyan Ching <xingyichengcyanching@gmail.com> wrote:

Dear Alister,

Thank you so much for the explanation and suggestion! I will keep in mind
there's an option to generate a 'fake' mdoc for Warp in case it is missing!

However, after I ran the mrc files through IMOD newstack, the problem
persists. I tried only specifying the input and output. I also tried to use
offsets and adjust the origin values. But the newly generated movies always
appeared grey and noisy.

As you could see in the attached images, the newly generated image was
flipped and shifted. However, the 'artificial cut' is still prominent, but
less centred.

Perhaps there is another way to go around this or I'm just not using the
newstack flags correctly?

Thank you so much for your help in advance!

Best,

Cyan

PhD student at Physical Chemistry Curie Lab



On Mon, 6 Dec 2021 at 12:58, Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Cyan,
>
> I can’t see your attachments but two things come to mind
>
> 1) for the ‘artificial cut’ there has been some discussion about warp
> mishandling some mrc file headers, running the files through newstack (from
> IMOD) or similar has fixed this issue for people
>
> 2) for handling tomograms in batch, warp expects movies for each tilt
> image to all be in the same directory, regardless of which tomogram they
> come from - it looks at mdoc files to see which images are part of the same
> tilt series
>
> Your filenames look like they were produced by Tomo5, if you’re using
> older data and don’t have mdoc files then
> https://github.com/alisterburt/mdocspoofer can be used to generate ‘fake’
> mdocs for warp processing.
>
> Cheers and good luck!
>
> Alister
>
> On 6 Dec 2021, at 11:45, Cyan Ching <xingyichengcyanching@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
> Dear Dimitry,
>
> Good morning!
>
> Is there perhaps a way to batch pre-process tens of tomograms of one
> acquisition using Warp? Does Warp require special file naming to
> distinguish frames of different tomograms?
>
> Following Warp-based pre-processing (motion and CTF correction) of a few
> tomograms (in mrc format, acquired on a TF Glacios 200kV cryo-transmission
> electron microscopes (Cryo-TEM) equipped with Falcon 3 direct electron
> detectors), white lines appeared across thon rings in the Fourier space and
> individual tilts in real space seemed artificially cut as it is shown in
> the attached images. Each movie of the frames is named as:
> Position_1_001[-9.00]_Fractions.mrc. Is there an explanation for this? Do
> you have any suggestions on how this could be fixed?
>
> Thank you so much for your help in advance!
>
> Wish you a great day!
>
> Best,
>
> Cyan Ching
>
> PhD student at the Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
>
>

> .
>
>
